title: $220M Capital Stack, Shelter Rent PILOT Agreement, Green Space Concerns, and East Campus Future Timeline
summary: Commissioner Collins describes east campus as physically infeasible for safe operation -- camera-height ceilings allow vandalism, mid-century 'poor planning' design, security costs exceed what property can cash-flow. Councilor Patrona Jones-Rowser raises green space concerns about concentrating 393 residents into smaller footprint. Council clarifies geographic terminology -- 'hilltop' (east campus) vs 'lower east side' (near Kennedy Square) are distinct communities requiring separate outreach. Total project cost: $220M ($125M direct construction, remainder in architect/engineer fees, loan interest, permits). Capital stack: $75M from HCR (tax-exempt bonds + subsidy), ~$100M from federal and state LIHTC, ~$45M in non-cash sources (accrued interest, assumed HCR debt extensions). One-for-one 393-unit replacement; bedroom mix unchanged per HUD regulations. Current Mitchell Lama shelter rent yields $270-330K/year in property tax; new PILOT projected at ~$800K/year -- a net increase for the city. PILOT consistency required for senior mortgage and bond agency approval. East Adams comparison: 11-12 phases at $140M each. East campus future development at least 2 years out; developers and city will engage community on what site could become (open space, retail, more housing) during current construction period.

political_signal.description: Council pressing for green space preservation, full financial transparency, and concrete east campus timeline

processing_notes: DPD in detected_departments is contextual (permits reference); actual department is Neighborhood and Business Development
Mitchell Lama = NYS affordable housing program providing shelter rent tax abatement; PILOT = Payment In Lieu Of Taxes
East Adams/Pioneer Homes = major Syracuse public housing redevelopment project on south side, used as comparison benchmark
$140M figure refers to per-phase East Adams cost, not this project; $220M is Parkside total
Segment 5 of 7 from Economic Development Committee Meeting, January 22nd 2026
